metadata.title: Studio Apartment
metadata.description: Single-room residential unit where living, sleeping, and cooking occupy one open space. Designed for maximum spatial efficiency.

body.purpose: Provide a self-contained, affordable living unit for a single occupant or couple in high-density urban contexts where land cost demands maximum floor-area efficiency.
body.planning_philosophy: Every square foot must serve at least one primary function. The plan is organised as a single open zone with a clearly separated wet core. Visual compression is countered through ceiling height, natural light, and unobstructed sightlines from entry to window.

occupancy.lifestyle_notes: Occupant spends significant time outside the unit. Storage and work-from-home provisions are secondary priorities.

required_spaces.name: living_sleeping_zone
required_spaces.notes: Combined living and sleeping area. Must accommodate a full-size bed, a seating area, and a work surface without spatial conflict.

required_spaces.name: kitchen_kitchenette
required_spaces.notes: Minimum two-burner hob, sink, and under-counter refrigerator. May be open to living zone or separated by a breakfast counter.

required_spaces.name: bathroom
required_spaces.notes: Combined wet room with WC, wash basin, and shower. Must be fully enclosed and ventilated.

required_spaces.name: entry_foyer
required_spaces.notes: Transition zone between corridor and living area. Provides shoe storage and coat hanging without entering the main space.

optional_spaces.name: dry_balcony
optional_spaces.notes: Laundry and drying space. Preferred over internal laundry to preserve living area.

optional_spaces.name: loft_sleeping_platform
optional_spaces.notes: Only viable when floor-to-ceiling height exceeds 13 ft. Frees ground-level area for living and work.

space_relationships.notes: Entry must transition directly into the main zone without passing through the kitchen or bathroom.
Open-plan or counter-separated kitchen creates perceived spaciousness.
Bathroom door must not open directly into the kitchen or onto the main seating area.
Wet room and food preparation must not share a wall with plumbing on the same face.

circulation.rule: A clear 3 ft movement corridor must exist from entry to window without furniture obstruction.
circulation.rationale: Prevents the unit from feeling like a corridor. Maintains the psychological sense of depth.

circulation.rule: The bathroom door must not swing into the main living zone.
circulation.rationale: An inward-swinging bathroom door in a compact unit creates collision risk and visual intrusion.

circulation.rule: The bed must be accessible from at least one long side and the foot end.
circulation.rationale: Minimum ergonomic requirement for making the bed and emergency egress.

ventilation.notes: Minimum one operable window. Cross-ventilation preferred where building orientation allows.
Exhaust fan mandatory if no operable window. Ducted extraction preferred over recirculating.
Exhaust fan mandatory. Minimum 6 air changes per hour. Duct to external face, not into ceiling void.

lighting.notes: Window must face an open aspect. Light well or internal courtyard is acceptable only if unobstructed.
Under-cabinet task lighting is mandatory regardless of natural light availability.
Waterproof IP44-rated fixtures above wash basin. Separate switched circuit from main living zone.

engineering.constraint: Minimum floor-to-floor height 10 ft to allow 9 ft clear ceiling after slab and finish.
All wet areas (bathroom, kitchen) must be grouped on a single wet wall to minimise pipe runs.
Minimum 4 double power outlets in the living-sleeping zone. Dedicated circuit for kitchen appliances.
Smoke detector mandatory in living-sleeping zone. Must be interconnected with building fire alarm system.
Minimum habitable room area 120 sqft per local building code. Studio must meet this as a single combined space.

construction.guideline: Use 100mm dense concrete block or double-layer gypsum board for party walls to achieve minimum STC 50.
Light-reflective finishes (LRV > 60) on walls and ceiling to counteract compact proportions.
Integrate full-height built-in storage along one wall. Minimum 30 sqft of storage volume.
Single continuous flooring material across living-sleeping zone and kitchen to visually expand the space.

constraints: Must include at least one operable window in the living-sleeping zone
Bathroom must be fully enclosed — open wet rooms are not permitted

validation_rules.condition: bathroom is present and fully enclosed
bathroom does not share a plumbing wall with kitchen
living_sleeping_zone has at least one natural light source
